calendar.matters: MOTION FOR STAY OF CIVIL PROCEEDING

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Continued To Aug-26-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR STAY OF CIVIL PROCEEDING
Off Calendar Feb-11-2026 Continued To Jun-30-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

MOTION FOR STAY OF CIVIL PROCEEDING.

The Hearing on the Status of Stay is before the court. Good cause apppearing and continuing to balance the factors considered in Avant! Corp. v. Superior Court (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 876, the court orders the stay is conintued in place until July 15, 2026, or futher order of the court. The court will hold a hearing on the status of the stay on June 30, 2026, at 9 am in this department. Each party must file s status report covering the status of the criminal action and any issues the party wants the court to consider in connection with the status of the stay no later than ten court days before the continued hearing, with courtesy copy provided to this department within 24 hours of filing. The court notes it did not receive courtesy copies of the reports filed in connection with this hearing which caused delay and waste of judicial resources. Non-compliance with this order is subject to sanctions.

Defendant is ordered to prepare a proposed order verbatim the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing. Non-compliance with this order, too, is subject to sanctions.

No proposed order received. Defendant failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Continued To Jul-02-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Of Civil Proceeding
Off Calendar May-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MAX ROTH's Motion For Stay Of Civil Proceeding.

Defendant Max V. Roth's motion for stay is GRANTED.

In Avant! Corp v. Sup. Crt, (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 876, 887, the court listed the following factors when ruling for consideration when adjudicating a request for a stay in a context akin to the circumstances of the instant case: (1) defendant's Fifth Amendment interest; (2) the interest of plaintiff in proceeding expeditiously and conducting discovery and the potential prejudice to plaintiffs of a delay; (3) the convenience of the court in the management of its cases, and the efficient use of judicial resources; (4) the interests of persons not parties to the civil litigation; and (5) the interest of the public in the pending civil and criminal litigation.

Upon considering these factors, the court stays prosecution of this action until further order of the court. The court continues this motion to January 15, 2026, to determine the status of the criminal action and whether a continued stay is warranted. By January 8, 2026, each side may file a supplemental brief up to 7 pages regarding the status of the criminal action or any other matter germane to continuance of the stay.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
David Ongaro, Esq. 415-433-3900 for Plaintiff.
Sarah Jane Truong, Esq. 707-525-8800 Perry Johnson Anderson Miller & Moskowitz LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED. Mr. Ongaro contests the denial of finding general and specific jurisdiction and further contests the court's denial of PLaintiff's request to conduct jurisdictional discovery. The Court shares its comments. Defense counsel Ms. Truong responds.
Mr. Ongaro requests one hour depositions. Ms. Truong responds; The Court has no jurisdiction over the parents. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTs MICHAEL ROTH,and VANESSA ROTH's Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

Specially-appearing Defendants Vanessa and Michael Roth's motion to quash under CCP 418.10(a)(1) is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
